world.title: Cole Tomas Allen in court; King Charles, Queen Camilla in Washington; Iran proposal on Strait of Hormuz reopening discussed by White House

world.title: Afghan official says Pakistani fire kills 7 along border
world.description: ASADABAD, Afghanistan: The Pakistani military killed at least seven civilians and wounded 85 others in an Afghan border province on Monday, a provincial health official said. Hundreds of people have been killed since the two neighbors went to war in late February, according to UN figures, although the violence has abated in recent weeks. world.title: Attacks shock Colombia just weeks before presidential election
world.description: A series of attacks in Colombia's southwest has raised security concerns before the May presidential election. Rebel groups have staged 26 attacks since Friday.

world.title: Indonesia train crash kills at least four people and injures dozens

business.description: The U.S. stock market’s record-breaking rally is slowing after uncertainty rose over the weekend about what will happen next in the Iran war.

business.title: Retailers Battling Swipe Fees Are Pushing Back on Settlement
business.description: Trade groups representing major retail chains including Walmart Inc. told a federal judge that they opposed a proposed $200 billion settlement in a longstanding antitrust suit against Visa Inc. and Mastercard Inc. over so-called swipe fees.

business.title: Consumers lost $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports
business.description: The agency reports that losses from social media scams have increased eightfold, and that social media scams resulted in higher losses than any other method scammers used to contact consumers.

science.title: The largest 3D map of the cosmos may alter the ultimate fate of the universe

science.title: Watch comet PANSTARRS 'switch on' its second tail as it makes closest approach to Earth
science.description: Comet PanSTARRS' ion tail became visible in satellite footage as it made its close approach to Earth on April 26.

science.title: In Cretaceous Oceans, Giant ‘Kraken-Like’ Octopuses May Have Been Top Predators
science.description: Massive, soft-bodied cephalopods up to 19 m (62 feet) long rivaled -- and perhaps hunted -- the ocean’s fiercest reptiles in the Cretaceous period, according to new research led by Hokkaido University paleontologists.

science.title: A Sixth Ocean Is Forming as East Africa Splits Apart

science.title: Mitochondria can spawn new ‘organelles’ - hinting at how modern cells evolved
science.description: Discovery bolsters theory that ancient mitochondria formed brand-new, specialized ‘sacs’ in cells by shedding their outer membranes.

science.title: ESA Heads to Arctic Sea Ice to Prepare Next
science.description: ESA scientists are testing instruments on Arctic sea ice to improve future Copernicus satellites that will track sea-ice loss and strengthen climate forecasts.

science.title: Shared signals shape phages’ lifestyles
science.description: Two back-to-back studies by Gallego-del-Sol et al. and Manley et al. demonstrate that arbitrium systems present in bacteriophages engage in cross-communication and modulate lysis–lysogeny decisions, with ecological implications.

ai.title: Sam Altman's sister loses lawyers in her sex-abuse lawsuit against OpenAI CEO

ai.title: NVIDIA's Rubin Lands Inside Google's Virtual Machine, Stretching Multi Site Clusters to Nearly 1 Million GPUs
ai.description: Google and NVIDIA have teamed up to provide users with access to as much as one million NVIDIA GPUs to power up the freshly launched A5X instances. The announcement is part of the pair's latest collaboration to reduce inference costs and improve token throughput. Their A5X system relies on NVIDIA's network accelerators that enable the development of single and mutli-cluster computing infrastructure for AI workloads. NVIDIA & Google to Allow Connecting Nearly a Million Rubin AI GPUs For A5X Instances Through Latest Collaboration The A5X instances are Google's latest products that are designed specifically to run agentic artificial intelligence workloads. ai.title: Child advocates call for online harms bill covering AI chatbots, gaming
ai.description: OTTAWA — Ottawa can't afford to wait any longer to introduce new online harms legislation that covers AI chatbots and video games, children’s advocates and about a dozen kids told a press conference on Parliament Hill Monday.
